name: Kualoa Beach Park
tagline: Chinaman's Hat sits right offshore, the Ko'olau mountains tower behind you — this might be the most scenic beach on the island.
parking: Large free lot right at the park
bestTime: Morning for best light on the mountains; weekdays to avoid crowds
talkStory: Kualoa means 'long back' in Hawaiian and was one of the most sacred places on Oahu — ancient chiefs brought their children here to be educated, and it was a pu'uhonua (place of refuge). Mokolii island offshore (nicknamed Chinaman's Hat) is said to be the tail of a mo'o (lizard) slain by the goddess Hi'iaka. At low tide you can wade across the sandbar to the island, but check the tide chart — people get stranded when the water rises. Jurassic Park, 50 First Dates, and Kong: Skull Island were all filmed at Kualoa Ranch directly behind you.
